rubric_results.check: Title is non-empty and plausibly the document's title?
rubric_results.note: The value is a substantive title referencing a 1980 Congressional report on renewable resources with an EIS component, which is plausible.

rubric_results.check: Length is reasonable (not a sentence fragment, not the whole abstract)?
rubric_results.note: The title is a single descriptive phrase of appropriate length for a government document title.

rubric_results.check: If two sources (NUL + Haiku) — do they roughly agree?
rubric_results.note: Only one source (NUL) was provided; no Haiku source to compare.

title.notes: The extracted title is plausible and well-formed for a government EIS document. However, only one source (NUL) was cited and no page text was provided to verify the extraction, which limits confidence. The title follows a standard government document naming convention and appears credible, but independent verification from cited page content would strengthen confidence.

rubric_results.check: Year falls within 1969 ≤ year ≤ current year?
rubric_results.note: 2026 is in the future and exceeds the current year (2025), failing the upper bound check

rubric_results.check: Year present in NUL metadata OR found on first 3 pages?
rubric_results.note: NUL metadata reportedly returns 2026, though no cited page text was provided to verify regex finding of 1980

rubric_results.check: If NUL and regex disagree, value reflects this with a low-confidence flag?
rubric_results.note: Extraction correctly flags the NUL=2026 vs regex=1980 disagreement with low confidence

year.notes: The extracted year of 2026 fails the validity range check as it is a future year beyond the current date. The regex independently found 1980 on the first 3 pages, which is a plausible EIS publication year and likely the correct value. No cited page text was provided to adjudicate between the two sources, and the NUL metadata value of 2026 appears erroneous. A human reviewer should confirm the correct year, with 1980 being the more credible candidate.

rubric_results.check: Type is one of: Draft, Final, Supplemental, ROD, Unknown?
rubric_results.note: Final is a valid EIS type from the allowed list.

rubric_results.check: Regex hit on first page and Sonnet on first 2 pages agree?
rubric_results.note: No regex source is reported; only Sonnet (first 2 pages) is cited. Cannot confirm agreement between methods.

rubric_results.check: Evidence phrase supports the chosen type?
rubric_results.note: No cited pages or evidence phrases were provided to support the 'Final' classification.

eis_type.notes: The extracted value of 'Final' is plausible but cannot be verified because no cited page text or evidence phrase was provided, and only a single extraction source (Sonnet) is reported with no regex corroboration. The medium confidence assigned by the extractor combined with the absence of supporting evidence warrants human review before accepting this classification.

rubric_results.check: All listed agencies appear in NUL contributors OR on cited title pages?
rubric_results.note: United States. Forest Service is sourced from NUL and is a well-recognized standard authority form for this agency.

rubric_results.check: Joint-lead (>2 agencies) flagged for human review?
rubric_results.note: Only one agency listed; joint-lead check not applicable.

rubric_results.check: No role text left in agency names?
rubric_results.note: No parenthetical role text such as '(issuing body)' is present in the extracted value.

lead_agency.notes: The single extracted agency 'United States. Forest Service' is in standard authority form, sourced from NUL, and contains no extraneous role text. No cited title pages were provided, but the NUL source is sufficient for verification. No joint-lead concern applies here.

rubric_results.check: project_description text supported by cited pages
rubric_results.note: Cited pages confirm: Forest and Rangeland Renewable Resources Planning Act of 1974, amended by NFMA 1976, 187 million acres NFS, four major responsibilities, 12 program elements including recreation/wilderness/wildlife/fish/range/timber/water/minerals, High Bound and Low Bound structure — all visible in the Introduction and Chapter 1 text.

rubric_results.check: Numbers, places, dates match cited pages
rubric_results.note: 187 million acres confirmed in Introduction; 1981-2030 planning period confirmed; High/Low Bound terminology confirmed; 12 program elements confirmed; 1974 Act and 1976 NFMA amendment confirmed.

rubric_results.check: affected_community text supported by cited pages
rubric_results.note: Rural/nonmetropolitan communities, timber-dependent communities, ranching communities, nonindustrial private forest landowners, State forestry agencies, county governments, Native Americans, minorities, youth/elderly/disadvantaged via Human Resource Programs — all referenced in the cited pages content.

rubric_results.check: alternatives_overview text supported by cited pages
rubric_results.note: The cited pages provided do not contain detailed descriptions of the five alternatives (Alt 1-5) or Alternative 4a. The Table of Contents references Chapter 3 for alternatives, but the actual Chapter 3 text is not among the pulled-in cited pages. The specific characterizations of each alternative cannot be verified from the visible content.

rubric_results.check: environmental_impact text supported by cited pages
rubric_results.note: Clean Water Act goals, wildlife habitat improvement percentages (mule deer 20%, white-tailed deer 20%, black-tailed deer 25%, elk 18%, turkey 33%, etc.), anadromous fish 30% increase at High Bound, and Low Bound species declines are all confirmed in Chapter 1 Part I text visible in the cited pages.

rubric_results.check: public_response based_on_main_doc_only=true and limited to main doc
rubric_results.note: The public_response field is marked based_on_main_doc_only=true and references content from the main RPA document. The Preface and Introduction confirm public involvement sessions nationwide and written/oral comments.

rubric_results.check: public_response specific figures (2,000 pages, 1,600 respondents, 70%, 45%, 150 witnesses) supported by cited pages
rubric_results.note: The visible cited page content does not contain these specific statistics. The Preface mentions public involvement generally but does not provide the specific numbers cited (2,000 pages of input, 1,600 respondents, 70% unaffiliated, 45% preferred an alternative, 150 Congressional witnesses). These would need to come from Appendix B (Public Involvement) which is not in the pulled-in text.

rubric_results.check: No invention or hallucinated detail beyond cited content
rubric_results.note: Specific quantitative claims in public_response (2,000 pages, 1,600 respondents, 70%, 45%, preference breakdowns by region, 150 witnesses) and specific alternative characterizations in alternatives_overview cannot be verified from the visible cited pages and may be hallucinated or drawn from pages not shown.

summary.notes: The project_description and environmental_impact fields are well-supported by the visible cited content. However, the alternatives_overview contains detailed characterizations of five alternatives plus Alternative 4a that are not visible in the pulled-in pages (Chapter 3 text is absent). The public_response field contains highly specific statistics (2,000 pages, 1,600 respondents, 70% unaffiliated, 45% preferred an alternative, 150 Congressional witnesses, regional preference breakdowns) that do not appear in any of the visible cited pages — these likely come from Appendix B which was not pulled in. Re-extraction is needed with the actual Chapter 3 and Appendix B pages included to verify or correct these claims.

rubric_results.check: Each listed alternative is actually discussed on its cited page?
rubric_results.note: The cited text (pages 136-215 range) includes the State of Utah comments section which explicitly discusses Alternatives 1 through 5 with descriptions matching the extracted content.

rubric_results.check: No Action alternative included if cited text includes it?
rubric_results.note: The cited text does not include a 'No Action' alternative; the document presents five numbered program direction alternatives, none labeled 'No Action.'

rubric_results.check: Names and descriptions match terminology used in the cited text?
rubric_results.note: Alternative 1-5 names and descriptions closely mirror the Utah state comments text. Minor elaborations in the extraction (e.g., 'moderate levels of market and non-market resources' for Alt 3, 'recommended for recreation, wildlife...' for Alt 3) go slightly beyond the literal Utah comment text but are consistent with the broader document context.

rubric_results.check: No outside-knowledge alternatives added that aren't in the chapter?
rubric_results.note: Only Alternatives 1-5 are listed, which are the alternatives discussed in the cited text. No extraneous alternatives were introduced.

alternatives.notes: The five alternatives are genuinely present and discussed in the cited text, primarily through the State of Utah comment letter. However, the source page range '136-215' is very broad and cannot be fully verified from the provided text excerpt, which only shows portions of the document. Some descriptive elaborations in the extraction (e.g., specific recommendations by program element for Alternative 3) appear to synthesize information from the Utah letter's 'Program Elements' section rather than from a standalone alternatives chapter, which is acceptable but worth noting.

rubric_results.check: Each chosen theme is supported by the doc summary fields?
rubric_results.note: No cited pages were provided, so direct textual support cannot be verified. The justification references summary fields but no actual text was pulled in to confirm.

rubric_results.check: Theme names come from the fixed taxonomy verbatim?
rubric_results.note: Cannot confirm taxonomy compliance without access to the fixed taxonomy list, but names appear plausible as standard EIS taxonomy entries.

rubric_results.check: Subthemes belong to the chosen theme(s) per the taxonomy?
rubric_results.note: 'Institutional Power and Resource Management' is ambiguously mapped — it could belong to Governance or Industrial Production, but without taxonomy reference this cannot be confirmed. 'Resource Extraction and Material Flows' under Industrial Production is reasonable.

rubric_results.check: 1–3 themes and 2–5 subthemes selected (within range)?
rubric_results.note: 3 themes and 4 subthemes — both within the allowed ranges.

themes.notes: No cited page text was provided, making it impossible to verify that the chosen themes and subthemes are grounded in the actual document content. Additionally, the subtheme 'Institutional Power and Resource Management' raises a cross-theme mapping concern that requires taxonomy verification. A human reviewer should confirm theme/subtheme alignment against the fixed taxonomy and the source document text.

rubric_results.check: Place names appear in cited pages?
rubric_results.note: Pages 1-30 contain extensive references to 'United States', individual states (Alaska, California, etc.), regions (Rocky Mountains, Pacific Northwest, Northeast, South), and specific National Forests, confirming nationwide scope.

rubric_results.check: is_multi_site set correctly?
rubric_results.note: is_multi_site is set to false, but the EIS explicitly covers all National Forest System lands, State and private forestry, and research stations across the entire country — multiple distinct sites and regions. It should be true.

rubric_results.check: For corridor projects: endpoints named, not just a midpoint?
rubric_results.note: This is a nationwide programmatic EIS, not a corridor project; endpoint check does not apply.

rubric_results.check: State abbreviations match U.S. conventions?
rubric_results.note: The extracted value uses no state abbreviations (state field is null for 'United States'), and abbreviations in the source text (WASH, MONT, OREG, CALIF, etc.) are consistent with U.S. Forest Service map conventions.

location.notes: The extracted location correctly identifies the United States as the geographic scope and the note accurately describes the nationwide programmatic nature of the EIS. However, is_multi_site is incorrectly set to false; the document explicitly covers hundreds of distinct National Forest System sites, research stations, and state/private forestry programs across all U.S. regions, which clearly constitutes a multi-site document. The field should be set to true before this extraction is accepted.

rubric_results.check: Each agency preparer named in the cited Preparers/Consultation text?
rubric_results.note: Richard Benjamin (RPA Program Manager), Bob Wray (Leader, Information Services, North Central Forest Experiment Station), and Rob Gibson (RPA Editor, Washington Office) are all explicitly named in the Acknowledgments section on page xvi. Thomas E. Hamilton (Director, Resources Program and Assessment) is named on the EIS cover page as the contact person.

rubric_results.check: Cooperating agencies / tribal nations actually listed in the cited text?
rubric_results.note: No cooperating agencies or tribal nations are listed in the cited text, and the extraction correctly reflects an empty list.

rubric_results.check: For public_commenters: stance present on cited page? Quote verbatim? Speaker clear?
rubric_results.note: No public commenters were extracted, and the cited text contains no individual public commenter entries with stances or quotes.

rubric_results.check: Private individuals: last-name-only or 'private commenter' convention followed?
rubric_results.note: No private individuals appear as commenters in the extraction or cited text.

key_people.notes: All four agency preparers are verifiably present in the cited text: Benjamin, Wray, and Gibson in the Acknowledgments (page xvi), and Hamilton on the EIS cover page. The empty cooperating agencies and public commenters fields are consistent with the cited text, which contains no such entries. The extraction accurately reflects the source material.
